extraction.one_line_summary: MAS Enrique won the Calar Alto summit by outsmarting LOPEZ Miguel Angel through superior corner positioning in the final 100m sprint.
tactical_patterns: YATES Simon attacked on penultimate climb to isolate GC rivals, forcing VALVERDE to chase at 32 seconds alone without teammates.
MAS studied 2012 Valverde victory footage repeatedly, pre-positioning before final corner to prevent LOPEZ from using his superior sprint.
SHAW Jonathan deliberately soft-pedaled to wait for VALVERDE rather than chase breakaway, revealing Sky team orders prioritizing podium defense.
LOPEZ Miguel Angel slammed tempo shut on corners to break rhythm of chase, demonstrating technical climbing superiority over raw power.

rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: Delivered knockout punch on penultimate climb after controlling entire race, showing Giro collapse taught restraint until finale
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: negative: Dropped at 32 seconds on final climb without teammates, lacking legs to respond when SHAW refused to help
rider_signals.MAS Enrique: positive: Pre-race video study translated to tactical positioning victory over faster finisher LOPEZ, revealing mature race craft
rider_signals.LOPEZ Miguel Angel: positive: Used corner accelerations as weapon to disrupt chasers but beaten by superior tactical preparation in final 100m
rider_signals.SHAW Jonathan: neutral: Tactical sacrifice for VALVERDE's podium defense exposed team orders, lost personal GC position for team goal
